sources.dataset: DOL/EBSA Form 5500 dataset (main form), plan year 2024
DOL/EBSA Form 5500 Schedule A (insurance), plan year 2024

method: Stream-filtered the all-filers datasets to this EIN + plan number, selected the most-recent filing by tax-period end, decoded line-8b welfare benefit codes via the DOL instructions, and aggregated the filing's Schedule A rows by arrangement. No values were modeled or imputed; absent items (e.g. Schedule C) are flagged, not filled.

evidence.claim: Participants are EMPLOYEES; covered lives (incl. dependents) are estimated at ~1.9× (children ≈ ¼ → covered adults ≈ 1.4×).

limitations: The participant count is the WHOLE-PLAN headcount from the main Form 5500 — captured regardless of funding, so it INCLUDES the self-funded population (here ~9× the fully-insured Schedule A lives). Self-insured headcount is not missing.
The 5500 does NOT report self-funded claims or spend; health burden is modeled from public data (Steps 2–3) and reconciled once a client census/claims feed arrives (Mode 1B).

Carrier/broker visibility comes from Schedule A/C. A purely self-insured plan with no insured carve-outs would show little here; Home Depot is a favorable example (31 carve-outs). Schedule C (advisor of record) not attached here.
Annual filing — figures are ~1 year old by the time they publish.
